factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to production delays at automotive SCS customers.

evidence.text: Lower than expected revenue growth due to production delays at our automotive SCS customers, primarily related to the worldwide semiconductor supply shortage.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to production delays, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Pricing power is challenged due to inaccurate key assumptions in contracts.

evidence.text: Our key assumptions and pricing structure of our SCS and DTS contracts prove to be inaccurate.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Inaccurate key assumptions in contracts challenge pricing power, leading to a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining due to production delays.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes due to production delays suggest a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to new regulations.

evidence.text: Higher prices for vehicles, diesel engines and fuel as a result of new regulations.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs due to new regulations present a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising due to shortages and potential turnover.

evidence.text: Difficulties in attracting and retaining drivers and technicians due to driver and technician shortages, which may result in higher costs to procure drivers and technicians and higher turnover rates affecting our customers.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to shortages and potential turnover indicate a moderate headwind.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain disruptions due to semiconductor shortages create a moderate headwind.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Management shows confidence through share buybacks.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2022, we purchased an aggregate of 197,094 shares of our common stock in employee-related transactions.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. UNREGISTERED SALES OF EQUITY SECURITIES AND USE OF PROCEEDS

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is evident through share buybacks, indicating a very positive development.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and interest rates.

evidence.text: Higher borrowing costs. Unanticipated interest rate and currency exchange rate fluctuations.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are active through buyback programs.

evidence.text: In October 2021, our Board of Directors authorized two new share repurchase programs.
